patterns.title: Runtime artifact dirty blocks integration
patterns.summary: Generated orchestrator runtime artifacts can leave main dirty and block runner or integration prechecks.
symptoms: main worktree is not clean after dry-run or dispatch
agent-cycle execute precheck stops on ops/agent-orchestrator/runs artifacts
integration is blocked even though no business file changed
root_causes: dry-run or dispatch wrote generated plan, prompt, queue, or event artifacts
precheck treated orchestrator-generated artifacts the same as business dirty files
recommended_improvements: Auto-commit event-first dispatch artifacts before runner execution
Doctor should classify runtime artifacts separately from business dirty files

patterns.title: Completed run missing queue/result finalization
patterns.summary: Agent run logs and result artifacts show success, but task queue/result state remains CLAIMED or READY.
symptoms: task result is MISSING or not DONE
task status remains CLAIMED or READY after successful agent work
root_causes: complete-task.mjs was not invoked by the worker
event/read-model rebuild did not materialize result status
reconcile did not trust successful run log plus committed evidence
matching_hints: run.log exit 0 but task result is not DONE
recommended_improvements: Use truthful reconcile from run log, agent commit, report, and result artifact
Surface missing completion as a doctor finding before integration

patterns.title: Agent idle because router rule is missing
patterns.summary: Natural-language work can fail to reach the right agent when domain keywords or fallback rules are incomplete.
symptoms: agent is idle while related work exists
owner assignment is manual or inconsistent
unknown domain falls back without useful reason
root_causes: planner does not explain owner selection
agent registry and router rules are not fully unified
recommended_improvements: Route unknown work to agent-5 planning
Add routing smoke tests for common natural-language requests

patterns.title: Duplicate lock blocks task execution
patterns.summary: Duplicate or stale locks make READY/CLAIMED state ambiguous and can block otherwise runnable agents.
symptoms: duplicate lock entries for one task and agent
DONE task still has an active lock
CLAIMED task has no matching clean runnable state
root_causes: legacy JSON lock writes were not idempotent
completed tasks did not clear locks
manual reconcile preserved old lock rows
matching_hints: DONE task still has active lock

patterns.title: Queue and event read-model inconsistency
patterns.summary: Event store and compatibility JSON can drift, which makes dispatch, audit, and doctor disagree.
symptoms: event queue read model differs from task-queue.json
event lock read model differs from task-locks.json
event result read model differs from task-results.json
root_causes: not every status write path was event-first
rebuild/read-model apply was skipped after event writes
legacy JSON was edited directly during reconcile
recommended_improvements: Move remaining write paths to event-first
Keep doctor event health visible in every cycle
